_comment: TEMPLATE — copy to evidata_config.json and fill in. This .example file is safe to commit; the real evidata_config.json should NOT be committed if it ever contains environment-specific values. No secrets live in either file — only env-var names.
_comment_enabled: Master switch. false (or this whole file absent) => preflight skipped silently, pipeline behaves as if EviData never existed. true => preflight runs and a failure aborts the whole project.
_comment_endpoints: Replace <evidata-host> with the FQDN of the EviData VM. Same host for both ports: 8000 = EviData API, 3000 = Keycloak. Use https:// if the deployment terminates TLS.
_comment_client_id: Keycloak client id. 'evidata-api' is the install-doc default; confirm with whoever deployed the stack.
_comment_secrets: These are env-var NAMES, not values. Set the actual values as environment variables on the host that runs the pipeline. Required by EviDataClient::authenticate() — pipeline throws if any of the three env vars is empty.
_comment_polling: Async report generation tuning. poll_interval_seconds = gap between status checks; poll_timeout_seconds = give-up deadline. Both optional (EviDataClient defaults: 10 / 600).
_comment_report_metadata: Static labels stamped on every report. analysis_type also sent with the dataset upload. All optional (EviDataClient supplies defaults).
_comment_qis: REQUIRED when enabled=true. Quasi-identifier column names — must EXACTLY match the clinical CSV/TSV headers (case-sensitive). resolveEvidataConfig() throws if this is missing or not an array.
_comment_population_size: Reference population size. Must be >= (CSV rows + 1). Optional (default 50000).
_comment_from_address: Optional. From: address on the privacy-failure email. Omit to fall back to archimedes-pipeline@<hostname>.
